---
format:
  pdf:
    lang: en
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    keep-tex: false
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    mainfont: "Arial"
    fontenc: "T1"
    inputenc: "utf8"
    fontsize: 10pt
    title-block-style: none
    colorlinks: true
    urlcolor: blue
    linkcolor: blue
    geometry: "top=1.5cm, bottom=1.5cm, left=2cm, right=2cm"
    papersize: letter
    microtype: true
    linestretch: 1.0
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \usepackage[normalem]{ulem}
        \newcommand{\cvsection}[1]{%
          \vspace{-1.5em}
          \section*{\fontsize{11}{13}\selectfont\underline{\makebox[\linewidth][l]{#1}}}%
          \vspace{-1.3em}
        }
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \singlespacing
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \setlist{itemsep=2pt, parsep=0pt, topsep=0pt, partopsep=0pt}
---

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
  {\large \textbf{Luis E. Mart\'{i}nez-Rodr\'{i}guez, Ph.D.}}\\[0.5ex]
  \normalsize
  Boston, MA \,\textbar\, US Work Authorized \,\textbar\, luised94@mit.edu \\
  \href{https://linkedin.com/in/luis-martinez-1394b619a}{linkedin.com/in/luis-martinez-1394b619a} \,\textbar\, \href{https://github.com/luised94}{github.com/luised94}
\end{center}

\cvsection{Summary}

Postdoctoral researcher with broad expertise in assay development, protein biochemistry, and NGS workflows. Designed and validated multi-step enzyme assays, purified multi-subunit protein complexes, and built genomic analysis pipelines from scratch. Seeking to apply scientific versatility and systematic approach to developing innovative custom solutions in the biotechnology sector.

\cvsection{Skills}

\textbf{Biochemistry:} protein purification (bacterial/yeast, multi-subunit complexes, FPLC and ÄKTA method development), reconstituted eukaryotic replication assays, EMSA, ATPase assays, fluorescence polarization

\textbf{Genomics:} ChIP-seq (sample preparation through peak calling and motif analysis), whole-genome sequencing, variant calling, NGS library preparation

\textbf{Computation:} R (Gviz, genomic visualization), Bash (NGS pipelines on Linux HPC: Bowtie2, SAMtools, deeptools), Python, Git/GitHub, documentation and workflow development

\textbf{Genetics:} genetic screen design and validation, CRISPR-Cas9 (yeast + mammalian), AID2 degron, tetrad dissection, bulk-segregant analysis

\cvsection{Experience}

\uline{MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (MIT)}, Cambridge, MA
\vspace{-0.1em}

\textbf{Post-doctoral Researcher,} Bell Lab, MIT Biology \hfill 2023--present

- Designed and optimized ChIP-seq workflows from sample preparation through computational analysis, establishing defined parameters and quality checkpoints at each step
- Built reproducible analysis pipeline from scratch (alignment, contamination filtering, signal normalization, peak calling, motif analysis) on Linux HPC with documentation for colleague training
- Evaluated and implemented multiple conditional depletion strategies (AID2 degron system) to enable acute loss-of-function experiments, selecting optimal approach based on systematic performance comparison

\textbf{Graduate Researcher,} Bell Lab, MIT Biology \hfill 2016--2023

- Designed and validated a genetic screen, then analyzed whole-genome sequencing data to identify causal variants across multiple suppressor strains
- Developed and optimized quantitative enzyme assays (ATPase activity, DNA binding, fluorescence polarization) to characterize the biochemical effects of mutations on multi-protein complex function
- Reconstituted wild-type and mutant multi-subunit complexes from purified components, optimizing expression and purification protocols across bacterial and yeast systems

\textbf{Research Intern,} MIT/Pfizer Internship Program (Course 7.930[J]) \hfill 2022

- Operated the Beacon Optofluidic System for high-throughput single-cell screening of CRISPR-edited primary human cells, working within partner timelines on an unfamiliar platform
- Tested key assay parameters (conditioned vs.\ non-conditioned media, culture conditions) to optimize an immunofluorescent screening approach for therapeutic target identification

\cvsection{Education}

Ph.D. in Biology, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, 2023  
B.Sc. in Cellular and Molecular Biology, University of Puerto Rico-Río Piedras, 2016

\cvsection{Publications}

\textbf{Mart\'{i}nez-Rodr\'{i}guez, L.} \& Bell, S. P. (2026). A lethal ORC ATPase mutation is suppressed by alterations in ORC subunits and RNA Pol II transcription components. \textit{Submitted.}

\textbf{Mart\'{i}nez-Rodr\'{i}guez, L.}, Erdogan, O., Jimenez-Rodriguez, M., Gonzalez-Rivera, K., Williams, T., Li, L., \& Carter, C. W. (2015). Functional Class I and II Amino Acid-activating Enzymes Can Be Coded by Opposite Strands of the Same Gene. \textit{Journal of Biological Chemistry}, 290(32), 19710--19725.
